source_pr.title: test: fix the flaky test TestAuditPluginRetrying
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #65113

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

Add an internal retry for the trigger of `retry` mechanism. Sometimes we are too lucky to actually trigger the retry.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Use the following command to execute this test for multiple times:

```
go test -failfast -timeout 300000s  -run 'TestAuditPluginRetrying' -count=1000 -v ./pkg/server/tests/commontest
```

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: `TestAuditPluginRetrying` was flaky because the test expected the retry mechanism to be triggered by a single high-concurrency run, but sometimes the workload succeeded without retries (i.e., “too lucky”), making the assertion fail intermittently.
failure_signature: TestAuditPluginRetrying: intermittent assertion failure when retry is not triggered (e.g. `len(testResults) > concurrency` not satisfied)
root_cause_explanation: This test validates an audit plugin retry behavior by launching a highly concurrent UPDATE workload and then asserting that retries occurred. However, depending on timing/locking and the scheduler, that single run may complete without triggering retries, so the assertion can fail nondeterministically. The PR fixes the flakiness by wrapping the workload in `require.Eventually(...)`: it repeats the workload until the retry signal is observed (or times out), making the test robust against timing jitter.
fix_pattern: Avoid “one-shot” assertions for timing-dependent retry behavior; use `require.Eventually`/polling with a reasonable timeout to ensure the retry condition is actually triggered before asserting.

evidence: evidence: test: fix the flaky test TestAuditPluginRetrying
